title: Mildly Spicy Venezuelan Shungiku Paella
description: A pescatarian friendly Venezuelan dish featuring shungiku, perfect for a date night, cooked to perfection on a slate tile vessel.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large paella pan or slate tile vessel over medium high heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute, until fragrant.
Add the sliced mild spicy sausage and cook until browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the chopped shungiku and cook until it is wilted, about 3 minutes.
Add the smoked paprika and saffron threads, and stir to combine.
Add the uncooked white rice and stir to combine with the spices and shungiku mixture.
Add the chicken broth and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 20 minutes, or until the rice is cooked and the liquid has been absorbed.
Add the peeled and deveined shrimp and cook until they are pink and fully cooked, about 5 minutes.
Serve the paella hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
